project_pkg: Do not edit directly. Placeholder to be replaced by pre_prompt.py
author_name: Configure default with:  git config --global user.name "Your Name"
author_email: Configure default with:  git config --global user.email "Your email"

__prompts__.project_name: Project name, ex: my-project for README title, pyproject.toml, etc
__prompts__.project_pkg: Package name for python `import my_package` : Enter to accept project-derived default:
__prompts__.repo_slug: git_name as in github.com/mynamespace/git_name ` : Enter to accept project-derived default:
__prompts__.author_name: Name used in copyright, PyPi, git, etc
__prompts__.author_email: E-mail, Used in PyPi, git, etc, cannot be empty
__prompts__.repo_namespace: Repo namespace as in my_namespace in www.github.com/my_namespace/ :
__prompts__.auto_push_new_repo: Attempt to push new git repo automatically (requires ssh keys installed):
__prompts__.overwrite_repo:
OVERWRITE REMOTE REPO?  *****DANGEROUS****, will destroy existing remote repo. 
 Type YES to confirm:
__prompts__.install_command_entry_point: Yes if you are making a runnable program.  Can say no for library, but yes won't hurt:
__prompts__.create_wiki_page: Create wiki with home.md in branch wiki, and linked to main repo.
